free_text: Prompt fission gamma-ray spectrum characteristics from
Thin targets of 240Pu and 242Pu were placed
back-to-back at the central cathode position inside a
cylindrical twin Frisch grid ionization chamber.
The fission rates were 0.044/s and 0.54/s for 240Pu and
Number of fission events and number of gamma rays
detected in coincidence with a fission fragment:
Cylindrical twin Frisch grid ionization chamber.
The counting gas was methane at a pressure of
1.08*10**5 Pa at a flow-rate of about 90 ml/min,
providing a detection efficiency for fission fragments
(serial number A2987) of 76.2 mm diameter, 76.2 mm
length; mounted onto a photomultiplier tube (PMT)
from Hamamatsu (R10233-100-02) with a 89 mm diameter
quartz window. To detect gamma rays.
The pulse height of the gamma rays was calibrated with
standard radioactive sources with energies between
2614 keV) and AmBe (decay of the first excited state in

Standard unfolding of the measured PFGS was not
possible due to poor statistics. The so-called
transformation function was defined as ratio between
the emission spectrum and the measured spectrum and
The uncertainties of the transformation function
contain both the statistical ones from the measured
spectrum and the uncertainties of the emission
spectrum from the unfolding of the response function.
